figures.caption: Fig. 1. Illustration of a service robot operating in an open-world scenario. To fulfill tasks such as “heat up a chicken burger”, the robot must expand its abstract world model and account for uncertainty during planning.
Fig. 30. Detailed quantitative results for AI2THOR experiments.
Fig. 29. Detailed quantitative results for Block Processing World.
Fig. 22. Behavioral Analysis of Task 2: Place the remote with red button into the cabinet
Fig. 21. Behavioral Analysis of Task 1: Deliver a zero-sugar drink to the table.
Fig. 24. Behavioral Analysis of Task 4: Serve a heated chicken burger on the coffee table.
Fig. 25. Behavioral Analysis of Task 5: Throw away the blue-floral bowl and turn off the living room light.

Fig. 3. Overview of the HUME. The robot iteratively generates hypotheses to expand its model, plans with a model augmented by uncertain hypotheses, and executes actions to verify and update these hypotheses using newly acquired observations.
Fig. 18. Visualizations of all ProcTHOR house environments used in our experiments.
Fig. 27. Failure mode: Missing proactive search/verification (Baseline).
Fig. 26. Failure mode: Uncertain Model Expansion failure due to attribute misclassification.

Fig. 12. Snapshot of completing the task T5: Throw away a blue-floral bowl and make the living room light-off. The robot is required to locate the bowl, identify the blue-floral pattern, and determine how to turn off the living room light.
Fig. 13. Demonstration of a robot discovering microwave button functions through interaction.
